title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement codeofconduct@posit.co. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Contributing to glyfun
text: outlines propose change glyfun. detailed discussion contributing tidyverse packages, please see development contributing guide code review principles.

title: Contributing to glyfun
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to glyfun
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to glyfun
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("glycoverse/glyfun", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to glyfun
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to glyfun
text: Please note glyfun project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bin Fu. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Fu B (2026). glyfun: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://glycoverse.github.io/glyfun/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {glyfun: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis},   author = {Bin Fu},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://glycoverse.github.io/glyfun/}, }

title: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis
text: glyfun provides functional enrichment analysis glycoproteomics data. provides two sets functions, answering different questions: enrich_xxx(): “functions enriched proteins dysregulated glycosylation?” enrich_gc_xxx(): “functions enriched dyregulated glycan traits?” gc “glycan-centric”, means connecting glycan traits (e.g. core-fucosylation) functional annotations. Representation Analysis (ORA) Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) supported, common databases including GO, KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways, (Disease Ontology), NCG (Network Cancer Genes).

title: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis
text: glyfun package core glycoverse package. need install individually even installed meta-package glycoverse. Note: package still development productive use. Install development version (recommended):
code: pak::pkg_install("glycoverse/glyfun")

title: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis
text: 🚀 Get started: 📚 Reference:

title: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis
text: glyfun integrates seamlessly glydet (calculating site-specific derived traits motif quantification) glystats (differential expression analysis). final piece glycoproteomics analytical puzzle, glyfun bridges gap raw glycan quantification biological insight, enabling researchers move beyond data processing functional glycomining.

title: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis
text: Comming soon.
code: library(glyfun)

what: Helper function to prepare the universe parameter — detected_universe

text: function extracts detected proteins glyexp::experiment() glystats result. can readily passed universe parameter glyfun functions.

title: Helper function to prepare the universe parameter — detected_universe
code: detected_universe(x)

title: Helper function to prepare the universe parameter — detected_universe
text: x glyexp::experiment() glystats result.

title: Helper function to prepare the universe parameter — detected_universe
text: character vector protein UniProt IDs.

title: Helper function to prepare the universe parameter — detected_universe
code: library(glyexp) universe <- detected_universe(real_experiment) length(universe) #> [1] 4262 universe[1:5] #> [1] "P08185" "P04196" "P04196" "P04196" "P10909"

what: Glycan-Centric Disease Ontology (DO) Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_do
title: Glycan-Centric Disease Ontology (DO) Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_do
text: Performs glycan-centric Disease Ontology () -Representation Analysis (ORA). Instead traditional protein-centric enrichment, function links specific glycan traits disease associations. helps answer questions like "diseases enriched proteins specific dysregulated glycan motif?", grouping differential analysis results glycan traits computing disease enrichment trait.

title: Glycan-Centric Disease Ontology (DO) Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_do
code: enrich_gc_ora_do(   dea_res,   dea_p_cutoff = 0.05,   dea_log2fc_cutoff = c(-1, 1),   ont = "HDO",   organism = "hsa",   universe = NULL,   p_adj_method = "BH",   p_cutoff = 0.05,   q_cutoff = 0.2 )

title: Glycan-Centric Disease Ontology (DO) Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_do
text: dea_res Differential analysis result. Can one : Result glystats::gly_limma() (two groups), glystats::gly_ttest(), glystats::gly_wilcox(), called glyexp::experiment() "traitproteomics" type. tibble following columns: protein: Uniprot ID proteins trait: glycosylation trait (e.g. "TFc" proportion core-fucosylated glycans) site: glycosylation site. p_val: p-values, preferably adjusted p-values log2FC: log2 fold change dea_p_cutoff P-value cutoff statistical significance. Defaults 0.05. glystats result input, adjusted p-values used. dea_log2fc_cutoff Log2 fold change cutoff statistical significance. length-2 numeric vector, negative positive boundaries, respectively. example, c(-1, 1) means "log2FC < -1 log2FC > 1", c(-Inf, 1) means "log2FC > 1". Defaults c(-1, 1). ont One "HDO" (Human Disease Ontology), "MPO" (Mammalian Phenotype Ontology), "VDO" (Vector Disease Ontology). Passed ont DOSE::enrichDO(). Defaults "HDO". organism "hsa" (Homo sapiens) "mmu" (Mus musculus). Passed organism DOSE::enrichDO(). Defaults "hsa". universe Background genes Uniprot IDs, directly passed universe clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). NULL (default), genes data used. Another common pattern use detected proteins backgroud genes. can use detected_universe() help . p_adj_method Passed pAdjustMethod clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). p_cutoff Passed pvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). q_cutoff Passed qvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO().

title: Glycan-Centric Disease Ontology (DO) Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_do
text: list two elements: tidy_result: tibble enrichment results containing following columns: trait: Glycan trait id: term ID description: Term description gene_ratio: Ratio genes term total genes input bg_ratio: Ratio genes term total genes background rich_factor: Proportion term's total background genes found input fold_enrichment: Ratio gene_ratio bg_ratio (magnitude enrichment) z_score: Directional trend regulation (positive , negative ) p_val: Raw p-value hypergeometric test p_adj: Adjusted p-value q_val: Q-value (FDR) gene_id: Gene IDs term (separated "/") count: Number genes term raw_result: raw clusterProfiler clusterProfResult object list classes glyfun_gc_ora_do_res, glyfun_gc_ora_res, glyfun_res.

title: Glycan-Centric Disease Ontology (DO) Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_do
text: traditional glycoproteomics data analysis, usually perform differential expression analysis (DEA) glycoforms, extract proteins dysregulated glycosylation, perform functional enrichment (e.g. GO) proteins. enrich_xxx() functions (e.g. enrich_ora_go()). enrich_gc_xxx() functions differ link specific glycan traits functional annotations. Instead answering question "functions enriched dysregulated glycoproteins?", enrich_gc_xxx() answers questions like “functions enriched proteins dysregulated core-fucosylation?” Higher specificity, deeper insights. focusing distinct glycan motifs, helps pinpoint functional relevance specific glycosylation changes.

title: Glycan-Centric Disease Ontology (DO) Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_do
text: common pattern using function :
code: # 1. Use `glydet` to calculate derived traits or motif quantification. trait_exp <- derive_traits(exp)  # or `quantify_motifs()`  # 2. Perform differential analysis with `glystats`. dea_res <- gly_ttest(trait_exp)  # 3. Use this function. go_res <- enrich_gc_ora_go(dea_res)  # or other `enrich_gc_xxx()` functions

what: Glycan-Centric GO Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_go
title: Glycan-Centric GO Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_go
text: Performs glycan-centric Gene Ontology (GO) -Representation Analysis (ORA). Instead traditional protein-centric enrichment, function links specific glycan traits (e.g., core-fucosylation, sialylation) functional annotations. identifies biological functions significantly enriched proteins exhibiting specific glycosylation changes, grouping differential analysis results trait performing ORA.

title: Glycan-Centric GO Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_go
code: enrich_gc_ora_go(   dea_res,   dea_p_cutoff = 0.05,   dea_log2fc_cutoff = c(-1, 1),   orgdb = "org.Hs.eg.db",   ont = "MF",   universe = NULL,   p_adj_method = "BH",   p_cutoff = 0.05,   q_cutoff = 0.2 )

title: Glycan-Centric GO Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_go
text: dea_res Differential analysis result. Can one : Result glystats::gly_limma() (two groups), glystats::gly_ttest(), glystats::gly_wilcox(), called glyexp::experiment() "traitproteomics" type. tibble following columns: protein: Uniprot ID proteins trait: glycosylation trait (e.g. "TFc" proportion core-fucosylated glycans) site: glycosylation site. p_val: p-values, preferably adjusted p-values log2FC: log2 fold change dea_p_cutoff P-value cutoff statistical significance. Defaults 0.05. glystats result input, adjusted p-values used. dea_log2fc_cutoff Log2 fold change cutoff statistical significance. length-2 numeric vector, negative positive boundaries, respectively. example, c(-1, 1) means "log2FC < -1 log2FC > 1", c(-Inf, 1) means "log2FC > 1". Defaults c(-1, 1). orgdb Passed OrgDb clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). ont Passed ont clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). "BP", "MF", "CC", "". Defaults "MF". universe Background genes Uniprot IDs, directly passed universe clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). NULL (default), genes data used. Another common pattern use detected proteins backgroud genes. can use detected_universe() help . p_adj_method Passed pAdjustMethod clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). p_cutoff Passed pvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). q_cutoff Passed qvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO().

title: Glycan-Centric GO Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_go
text: list two elements: tidy_result: tibble enrichment results containing following columns: trait: Glycan trait id: Term ID description: Term description gene_ratio: Ratio genes term total genes input bg_ratio: Ratio genes term total genes background rich_factor: Proportion term's total background genes found input fold_enrichment: Ratio gene_ratio bg_ratio (magnitude enrichment) z_score: Directional trend regulation (positive , negative ) p_val: Raw p-value hypergeometric test p_adj: Adjusted p-value q_val: Q-value (FDR) gene_id: Gene IDs term (separated "/") count: Number genes term raw_result: raw clusterProfiler clusterProfResult object list classes glyfun_gc_ora_go_res, glyfun_gc_ora_res, glyfun_res.

title: Glycan-Centric GO Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_go
text: traditional glycoproteomics data analysis, usually perform differential expression analysis (DEA) glycoforms, extract proteins dysregulated glycosylation, perform functional enrichment (e.g. GO) proteins. enrich_xxx() functions (e.g. enrich_ora_go()). enrich_gc_xxx() functions differ link specific glycan traits functional annotations. Instead answering question "functions enriched dysregulated glycoproteins?", enrich_gc_xxx() answers questions like “functions enriched proteins dysregulated core-fucosylation?” Higher specificity, deeper insights. focusing distinct glycan motifs, helps pinpoint functional relevance specific glycosylation changes.

title: Glycan-Centric GO Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_go
text: common pattern using function :
code: # 1. Use `glydet` to calculate derived traits or motif quantification. trait_exp <- derive_traits(exp)  # or `quantify_motifs()`  # 2. Perform differential analysis with `glystats`. dea_res <- gly_ttest(trait_exp)  # 3. Use this function. go_res <- enrich_gc_ora_go(dea_res)  # or other `enrich_gc_xxx()` functions

what: Glycan-Centric KEGG Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_kegg
title: Glycan-Centric KEGG Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_kegg
text: Performs glycan-centric KEGG pathway -Representation Analysis (ORA). Instead traditional protein-centric enrichment, function links specific glycan traits biological pathways. helps answer questions like "pathways enriched proteins specific dysregulated glycan motif?", grouping differential analysis results glycan traits computing pathway enrichment trait.

title: Glycan-Centric KEGG Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_kegg
code: enrich_gc_ora_kegg(   dea_res,   dea_p_cutoff = 0.05,   dea_log2fc_cutoff = c(-1, 1),   organism = "hsa",   universe = NULL,   p_adj_method = "BH",   p_cutoff = 0.05,   q_cutoff = 0.2 )

title: Glycan-Centric KEGG Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_kegg
text: dea_res Differential analysis result. Can one : Result glystats::gly_limma() (two groups), glystats::gly_ttest(), glystats::gly_wilcox(), called glyexp::experiment() "traitproteomics" type. tibble following columns: protein: Uniprot ID proteins trait: glycosylation trait (e.g. "TFc" proportion core-fucosylated glycans) site: glycosylation site. p_val: p-values, preferably adjusted p-values log2FC: log2 fold change dea_p_cutoff P-value cutoff statistical significance. Defaults 0.05. glystats result input, adjusted p-values used. dea_log2fc_cutoff Log2 fold change cutoff statistical significance. length-2 numeric vector, negative positive boundaries, respectively. example, c(-1, 1) means "log2FC < -1 log2FC > 1", c(-Inf, 1) means "log2FC > 1". Defaults c(-1, 1). organism KEGG organism code. Passed organism clusterProfiler::enrichKEGG(). Defaults "hsa" (Homo sapiens). Common codes: "hsa" (human), "mmu" (mouse), "rno" (rat). universe Background genes Uniprot IDs, directly passed universe clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). NULL (default), genes data used. Another common pattern use detected proteins backgroud genes. can use detected_universe() help . p_adj_method Passed pAdjustMethod clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). p_cutoff Passed pvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). q_cutoff Passed qvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO().

title: Glycan-Centric Reactome Pathway Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_reactome
text: traditional glycoproteomics data analysis, usually perform differential expression analysis (DEA) glycoforms, extract proteins dysregulated glycosylation, perform functional enrichment (e.g. GO) proteins. enrich_xxx() functions (e.g. enrich_ora_go()). enrich_gc_xxx() functions differ link specific glycan traits functional annotations. Instead answering question "functions enriched dysregulated glycoproteins?", enrich_gc_xxx() answers questions like “functions enriched proteins dysregulated core-fucosylation?” Higher specificity, deeper insights. focusing distinct glycan motifs, helps pinpoint functional relevance specific glycosylation changes.

title: Glycan-Centric Reactome Pathway Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_reactome
text: common pattern using function :
code: # 1. Use `glydet` to calculate derived traits or motif quantification. trait_exp <- derive_traits(exp)  # or `quantify_motifs()`  # 2. Perform differential analysis with `glystats`. dea_res <- gly_ttest(trait_exp)  # 3. Use this function. go_res <- enrich_gc_ora_go(dea_res)  # or other `enrich_gc_xxx()` functions

what: Glycan-Centric WikiPathways Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_wp
title: Glycan-Centric WikiPathways Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_wp
text: Performs glycan-centric WikiPathways -Representation Analysis (ORA). Instead traditional protein-centric enrichment, function links specific glycan traits biological pathways. helps answer questions like "WikiPathways enriched proteins specific dysregulated glycan motif?", grouping differential analysis results glycan traits computing pathway enrichment trait.

title: Glycan-Centric WikiPathways Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_wp
code: enrich_gc_ora_wp(   dea_res,   dea_p_cutoff = 0.05,   dea_log2fc_cutoff = c(-1, 1),   organism = "Homo sapiens",   universe = NULL,   p_adj_method = "BH",   p_cutoff = 0.05,   q_cutoff = 0.2 )

title: Glycan-Centric WikiPathways Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_wp
text: dea_res Differential analysis result. Can one : Result glystats::gly_limma() (two groups), glystats::gly_ttest(), glystats::gly_wilcox(), called glyexp::experiment() "traitproteomics" type. tibble following columns: protein: Uniprot ID proteins trait: glycosylation trait (e.g. "TFc" proportion core-fucosylated glycans) site: glycosylation site. p_val: p-values, preferably adjusted p-values log2FC: log2 fold change dea_p_cutoff P-value cutoff statistical significance. Defaults 0.05. glystats result input, adjusted p-values used. dea_log2fc_cutoff Log2 fold change cutoff statistical significance. length-2 numeric vector, negative positive boundaries, respectively. example, c(-1, 1) means "log2FC < -1 log2FC > 1", c(-Inf, 1) means "log2FC > 1". Defaults c(-1, 1). organism WikiPathways organism name. Passed organism clusterProfiler::enrichWP(). Defaults "Homo sapiens". Use clusterProfiler::get_wp_organisms() see available organisms. universe Background genes Uniprot IDs, directly passed universe clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). NULL (default), genes data used. Another common pattern use detected proteins backgroud genes. can use detected_universe() help . p_adj_method Passed pAdjustMethod clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). p_cutoff Passed pvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). q_cutoff Passed qvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO().

title: Glycan-Centric WikiPathways Over Representation Analysis — enrich_gc_ora_wp
text: list two elements: tidy_result: tibble enrichment results containing following columns: trait: Glycan trait id: WikiPathways pathway ID description: Pathway description gene_ratio: Ratio genes pathway total genes input bg_ratio: Ratio genes pathway total genes background rich_factor: Proportion pathway's total background genes found input fold_enrichment: Ratio gene_ratio bg_ratio (magnitude enrichment) z_score: Directional trend regulation (positive , negative ) p_val: Raw p-value hypergeometric test p_adj: Adjusted p-value q_val: Q-value (FDR) gene_id: Gene IDs pathway (separated "/") count: Number genes pathway raw_result: raw clusterProfiler clusterProfResult object list classes glyfun_gc_ora_wp_res, glyfun_gc_ora_res, glyfun_res.

title: Disease Ontology (DO) Over Representation Analysis — enrich_ora_do
text: dea_res Differential analysis result. Can one : Result glystats::gly_limma() (two groups), glystats::gly_ttest(), glystats::gly_wilcox(), called glyexp::experiment() "traitproteomics" type. tibble following columns: protein: Uniprot ID proteins trait: glycosylation trait (e.g. "TFc" proportion core-fucosylated glycans) site: glycosylation site. p_val: p-values, preferably adjusted p-values log2FC: log2 fold change dea_p_cutoff P-value cutoff statistical significance. text: dea_res Differential analysis result. Can one : Result glystats::gly_limma() (two groups), glystats::gly_ttest(), glystats::gly_wilcox(), called glyexp::experiment() "traitproteomics" type. tibble following columns: protein: Uniprot ID proteins trait: glycosylation trait (e.g. "TFc" proportion core-fucosylated glycans) site: glycosylation site. p_val: p-values, preferably adjusted p-values log2FC: log2 fold change dea_p_cutoff P-value cutoff statistical significance. Another common pattern use detected proteins backgroud genes. can use detected_universe() help . p_adj_method Passed pAdjustMethod clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). p_cutoff Passed pvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO(). q_cutoff Passed qvalueCutoff clusterProfiler::enrichGO().

what: glyfun: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis — glyfun-package
title: glyfun: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis — glyfun-package
text: Provides functional enrichment analysis glycoproteomics data, including protein-centric glycan-centric approaches. 'enrich_xxx()' functions answer "functions enriched proteins dysregulated glycosylation?" (traditional protein-level enrichment), 'enrich_gc_xxx()' functions answer "functions enriched dysregulated glycan traits?" (glycan-centric, linking glycan traits like core-fucosylation functional annotations). Supports Representation Analysis (ORA) Gene Set Enrichment Analysis (GSEA) common databases including GO, KEGG, Reactome, WikiPathways, (Disease Ontology), NCG (Network Cancer Genes). Integrates seamlessly 'glydet' (site-specific derived traits) 'glystats' (differential expression analysis).

title: glyfun: Glycan-Centric Functional Enrichment Analysis — glyfun-package
text: Maintainer: Bin Fu 23110220018@m.fudan.edu.cn (ORCID)
